name: Queen Emma Summer Palace
tagline: The summer retreat of Queen Emma in the cool Nu'uanu Valley — intimate tours reveal the personal side of Hawaiian royalty.
bestTime: Weekday mornings for small tour groups and personal attention from docents
talkStory: Known in Hawaiian as Hanaiakamalama, this 1847 retreat in cool Nuuanu Valley was where Queen Emma and King Kamehameha IV escaped the heat of Honolulu. Emma was one-quarter English, deeply cosmopolitan, and founded Queen's Hospital in 1859 after watching her people die from Western diseases. After the monarchy fell, the home was slated for demolition until the Daughters of Hawaii rescued it in 1915 — they've maintained it ever since. The docents are often Daughters members themselves and share personal, passionate stories you won't get from a textbook.
